rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope of the annex.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for structural assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless the subcomponents and connections are properly represented.
Do not assume that AADL property names like Period or Deadline are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
